incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Count the bags. That's the first factor. Count the circles in each bag. That's the second factor.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on second attempt

params.description: All 9 bags highlight. Circles within each bag also highlight.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. There are 9 bags. Each bag has 5 circles. So it's 9 groups of 5. Place those two factors first, then skip count by 5s nine times to find the product.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Bags highlight sequentially as system counts by 5s. Numbers 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45 appear above bags in sequence.

steps.text: Let me show you how to build it. I count the bags: 9 bags. That's the first factor. I count the circles in each bag: 5 circles. That's the second factor. Now I skip count by 5s, nine times: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45. The product is 45. The complete equation is 9 times 5 equals 45.
